headline: 21 contracts and multisigs catalogued across Ethereum, Solana, Arbitrum, Optimism, and Base; identifies core controller, DAO governance, and shadow multisig used for merchant updates.

findings.text: Etherscan name-tag search for 'WBTC' and 'Wrapped BTC' confirmed the core token (0x2260...), Controller (0xCA06...), and Factory (0xe5a5...) addresses on Ethereum.
Official 'Transparency' dashboard at wbtc.network/transparency lists circulating supply and reserve verification endpoints, pointing to multi-chain deployments.
Audit link dashboard at wbtc.network/dashboard/audit contains historical reports from ChainSecurity and Solidified covering the original Ethereum smart contract suite.

GitHub repository 'WrappedBTC/DAO' provides a canonical list of DAO members and the deprecated vs. current multisig addresses (0xB33f...). 'WrappedBTC/bitcoin-token-smart-contracts' contains the Ethereum deployment logic.
Addresses for Solana (Hb5pJ...), Arbitrum (0x2f2a...), Optimism (0x68f1...), and Base (0x1de8...) were surfaced through a combination of GitHub documentation and block explorer cross-references.

rationale.verdict: Discovery completed across Ethereum, Solana, and L2s. Identified 21 core addresses including the active 8-of-13 DAO multisig and the 5-signer shadow multisig reported to have bypassed governance for HTX merchant addition.

evidence.shows: Verified WBTC Token contract on Ethereum.
Canonical registry of DAO members, current multisig (0xB33f...), and deprecated multisig (0xD409...).
Shadow multisig (0x4dbb...) identified in November 2024 HTX merchant addition; signers 0x9778... and 0x5915... verified as non-listed participants.
Consolidated view of reserves and cross-chain supply verification.
WBTC Token on Arbitrum One.

unknowns: D6: Direct enumeration of 50+ merchants from the Members registry (0x3e86...) was skipped; registry address is recorded for downstream ingestion.

protocol_metadata.about: Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC) is an ERC-20 token on Ethereum backed 1:1 by Bitcoin. It enables Bitcoin liquidity to be used in DeFi protocols through a network of merchants and regulated custodians, governed by a multi-institutional DAO.

headline: 56 Ethereum addresses catalogued: 4 core contracts, 4 multisigs, 1 custodian, 46 active merchants, and 1 deprecated signer.

findings.text: Etherscan-grounded discovery surfaced the WBTC token and Controller; Ethereum was searched because the fetched official DAO repo and audit corpus identify the deployed WBTC control contracts on Ethereum.
The official WrappedBTC DAO README lists the current DAO multisig, deprecated multisig, WBTC token, token controller, factory, members contract, custodian, merchants, and DAO members.

The audit corpus was walked through the official deployment-verification page; ChainSecurity scope covers Controller, Factory, Members, and WBTC source files, while Solidified and Coinspect report pages were reachable but not parsed for deployed-address tables.

The commit-pinned bitcoin-token-smart-contracts repository contains deployment and verification artifacts; verify.js hardcodes the WBTC token address and discovers Controller, Members, Factory, merchants, and custodian from deployed state.
Pinned protocol.chains contains a single chain, Bitcoin; no multi-chain repeat was required, but the actual fetched smart-contract evidence is Ethereum.
The Factory contract was catalogued; its fetched ABI surface exposes mint and burn request methods rather than allPools, getPool, getMarket, or getVault child enumeration.

Every protocol_metadata.admin_addresses entry uses a controlled role prefix and an allowed actor_class value; multisig actor_class is used only where a fetched DeFiPunkd surfacer displayed a threshold.
Every address in protocol_metadata.admin_addresses traces to a fetched DeFiPunkd, Etherscan, official GitHub, or audit URL; failed-only sightings are left in unknowns.

rationale.verdict: Discovery has slice-defined grade unknown; the run catalogued 56 addresses from fetched Etherscan, DeFiPunkd, GitHub, and audit sources, while website-side deployment discovery, Bitcoin-native explorer discovery, several raw PDFs, and direct safe-owner API paths remained unresolved.

evidence.shows: Step 0 capability probe: web.run fetched the protocol website URL, but the parsed body had zero usable lines.

Step 0 surfacer probe succeeded. The Controller surfacer shows chain ethereum, pinned read block 25016155, getWBTC returning 0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599, owner returning 0x972Eed35781f09987a5c40F761f6A24623C570DE, members returning 0x3e8640574aa764763291eD733672D3A105107ac5, factory returning 0xe5A5F138005E19A3E6D0FE68b039397EeEf2322b, and write methods including transferOwnership and reclaimToken.

WBTC token surfacer shows chain ethereum, name Wrapped BTC, symbol WBTC, decimals 8, paused false, totalSupply 11802223227482 at pinned block 25016156, and owner returning the Controller address 0xCA06411bd7a7296d7dbdd0050DFc846E95fEBEB7.
Controller owner surfacer shows a 6-of-10 multisig at pinned block 25016156 and lists ten owners.

Members surfacer shows chain ethereum, custodian returning 0xb0F42D187145911C2aD1755831aDeD125619bd27, owner returning 0x4dbbbFb0e68bE9D8F5a377A4654604a62E851e80, and getMerchants returning 46 merchant addresses at pinned block 25016155.

Factory surfacer shows chain ethereum, owner and controller returning 0xCA06411bd7a7296d7dbdd0050DFc846E95fEBEB7, getMintRequestsLength returning 809, getBurnRequestsLength returning 487, and mint/burn request methods.
DAO multisig surfacer shows an 8-of-13 MultiSigWalletWithDailyLimit at pinned block 25016155 and lists thirteen owners.
Members owner surfacer shows a 5-of-9 MultiSigWalletWithDailyLimit at pinned block 25016155 and lists nine owners.
Custodian surfacer shows the address on Ethereum but reports no verified ABI found on chainId 1.

Deprecated DAO multisig surfacer shows an 11-of-18 MultiSigWalletWithDailyLimit at pinned block 25016155, lists eighteen owners including 0x36DEE7493Cb4fDEbbF6FD67BE4D4ec559aa61fe1, and exposes owner-management methods including addOwner, removeOwner, changeRequirement, confirmTransaction, and executeTransaction.
Etherscan token page identifies Wrapped BTC as an ERC-20 source-verified token, links wbtc.network, labels it Bitcoin Pegged, and describes WBTC as an ERC20 token backed 1:1 with Bitcoin.
Etherscan-grounded search result identifies the address as Wrapped BTC: Controller and references constructor argument token 0x2260fac5e5542a773aa44fbcfedf7c193bc2c599.

Official WBTC DAO README lists current multisig 0xB33f8879d4608711cEBb623F293F8Da13B8A37c5, old multisig 0xd409dB68d0E6E97E1cB1F3EB0BD38dE278bc3C68, WBTC token 0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599, token controller 0xca06411bd7a7296d7dbdd0050DFc846E95fEBEB7, factory 0xe5a5f138005e19a3e6d0fe68b039397eeef2322b, members 0x3e8640574aa764763291ed733672d3a105107ac5, custodian 0xb0f42d187145911c2ad1755831aded125619bd27, and merchant and DAO member lists.

Official deployment verification guide links ChainSecurity, Solidified, and Coinspect audits; records relevant WBTC smart-contract commit 0e8c140ab5fa006a17afa504dafcf311f56f81a2; and describes verification of deployed source code, owner configuration, custodian, merchants, and BTC custody coverage.
Commit-pinned smart-contract repository states it contains contracts implementing wrapped btc token and documents deployment through scripts/deployer.js.

Commit-pinned verification script reads Controller.sol, Factory.sol, Members.sol, and WBTC.sol; hardcodes WBTC address 0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599; discovers the Controller from WBTC.owner(); and reads Members, merchants, custodian, and Factory from deployed state.

ChainSecurity audit dated 2018-10-11 scopes Controller.sol, Factory.sol, Members.sol, and WBTC.sol; describes WBTC as an ERC20 representing BTC on Ethereum; describes minting when BTC is locked at a custodian and burning when WBTC is redeemed for BTC; and states the DAO has absolute power to change mint/burn and custodian-merchant management.
Solidified WBTC audit PDF page was reachable; filename indicates WBTC audit report dated 2018-11-19, but deployed-address tables were not parsed in this run.
Coinspect WBTC PDF page was reachable; filename indicates a WBTC report dated 2018-11, but deployed-address tables were not parsed in this run.
GitHub security page search result reported no SECURITY.md policy and no published security advisories for bitcoin-token-smart-contracts.

unknowns: D1: Bitcoin-native name-tag search was not completed because the checklist's canonical block-explorer list is EVM-oriented and no Bitcoin explorer search URL was provided.
D2: The protocol website fetched successfully but returned no usable parsed body, so a website-side deployed-contracts page could not be verified from wbtc.network in this run.
D3: Solidified and Coinspect PDF pages were reachable, but raw PDF extraction did not yield scope address tables in this run.
D5: Pinned protocol.chains lists Bitcoin, while all fetched deployed smart-contract addresses are Ethereum; no additional Bitcoin contract address corpus was discovered.

D6: Factory child enumeration via allPools, getPool, getMarket, and getVault was attempted through DeFiPunkd read URLs but URL normalization was rejected by the fetch allowlist; the fetched factory surfacer showed no such enumeration methods.

D8: Direct /api/safe/owners fetches for 0x972Eed35781f09987a5c40F761f6A24623C570DE, 0xB33f8879d4608711cEBb623F293F8Da13B8A37c5, and 0xD409dB68d0E6E97E1cB1F3EB0BD38dE278bc3C68 normalized parameter order and were rejected by the fetch allowlist; multisig thresholds were taken from fetched DeFiPunkd surfacer pages instead.
D8: No current public bug bounty URL or private security contact was verified; search results showed no SECURITY.md for bitcoin-token-smart-contracts and no WBTC HackerOne hit.

admin_addresses.role: multisig (official WBTC DAO README current multisig, 8-of-13)

protocol_metadata.about: WBTC is an ERC-20 token on Ethereum that represents Bitcoin and is described by Etherscan as backed 1:1 with Bitcoin. The ChainSecurity audit describes the mechanism as minting WBTC when the corresponding BTC is locked at a custodian and burning WBTC when BTC is redeemed. The fetched contracts include a token, Controller, Factory, and Members registry; the Members contract surfaces the custodian and active merchant list.

headline: 15 addresses catalogued across Ethereum (11) and 4 L2 chains; on-chain Controller owner (0x972E) diverges from disclosed 8-of-13 DAO multisig; Members contract owned by shadow 5-of-7 multisig.

findings.text: Etherscan name-tag search confirmed WBTC ERC-20 token (Wrapped BTC: WBTC Token) at 0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599 on Ethereum; deployer tagged 'Wrapped BTC: Deployer' at 0x8b41783ad99fcbeb8d575fa7a7b5a04fa0b8d80b. Per-chain explorer searches found bridged WBTC on Arbitrum (arbiscan), Optimism (optimistic.etherscan), Polygon (polygonscan), and a LayerZero OFT version on Base (basescan). All 8 pinned address_book entries confirmed present on Ethereum.

Official deployments reference is https://github.com/WrappedBTC/DAO/blob/master/README.md, listing WBTC token, Controller, Factory, Members, current DAO multisig (0xB33f), deprecated DAO multisig (0xD409), and custodian (BitGo, 0xb0F4). The wbtc.network website returned an empty body (JS-rendered); no separate docs URL verified this run.

Audit summary at WrappedBTC/bitcoin-token-smart-contracts/docs/audit_summary.md identifies three 2018 audits: ChainSecurity, Solidified (2018-11-19), Coinspect (filename implies 2018-11-13). Scope covers WBTC token, Controller, Members, Factory at commit 0e8c140ab5fa006a17afa504dafcf311f56f81a2. Audit PDFs not directly fetched in this run (D3-offchain).

WrappedBTC GitHub org has 3 public repos: bitcoin-token-smart-contracts (contracts + audits), DAO (governance, addresses, merchant list), website. Audit commit 0e8c140ab5fa006a17afa504dafcf311f56f81a2. DAO/README.md is the canonical address index; no hardhat/foundry deployment JSON files encountered.

4 additional chains with WBTC-branded tokens confirmed from explorer search results: Arbitrum (0x2f2a2543, BeaconProxyFactory-style bridge), Optimism (0x68f180fc, L2 StandardERC20 with l1Token=0x2260), Polygon (0x1bfd6703, UChildERC20 proxy), Base (0x0555E30d, WBTCOFT LayerZero OFT). L2 tokens are bridge-issued and not governed by the WBTC DAO; surfacer URLs for these rejected (D8, below).
Factory (0xe5a5f138) handles merchant mint/burn requests; it does not deploy child contracts or pools. Factory owner() = Controller (0xCA06), confirmed on-chain. No factory-enumeration applicable.

Role taxonomy applied to all 11 Ethereum addresses: WBTC token (token), Controller (owner), DAO multisig 0xB33f (Gnosis Safe, 13 signers confirmed, threshold 8-of-13 per GitHub PR #12 and news sources, not confirmed on-chain this run), shadow/Small-DAO multisig 0x4dbb (MultiSigWalletWithDailyLimit, owns Members, BitGo holds 3-of-5 confirmed keys), deprecated DAO multisig 0xD409 (MultiSigWalletWithDailyLimit, deprecated), Factory (factory), Members (other), BitGo custodian 0xb0F4 (no ABI on Etherscan, actor_class unknown), current Controller owner 0x972E (unknown, see D8), deployer EOA 0x8b41 (eoa). 24 merchant addresses listed in DAO/README.md not individually enumerated per D6 scope cap.

Two addresses lack surfacer fetch confirmation: (1) 0x972Eed35781f09987a5c40F761f6A24623C570DE—returned by DeFiPunkd read API as current Controller owner; surfacer URL rejected; actor_class unknown. This diverges from the DAO/README claim that the DAO multisig owns Controller. (2) 0x8b41783ad99fcbeb8d575fa7a7b5a04fa0b8d80b—WBTC token deployer EOA, from fetched Etherscan page; surfacer URL would be non-defipunkd-generated, rejected. Both recorded in admin_addresses and unknowns[]. L2 token addresses (4) also in D8 since sourced from search snippets.

rationale.verdict: Discovery walk covered: (1) DeFiPunkd read-API on-chain calls for all 8 pinned addresses confirming ABIs, ownership, and member lists; (2) Etherscan full page for the WBTC token contract; (3) GitHub WrappedBTC/DAO README for governance addresses, 13 DAO member identities, and 24 merchant list; (4) GitHub WrappedBTC/bitcoin-token-smart-contracts audit summary; (5) per-chain explorer searches for L2 deployments. 15 addresses catalogued: 11 Ethereum + 4 L2 token contracts. Critical extension: Controller owner is 0x972E (not the claimed DAO multisig 0xB33f); Members is owned by the 5-of-7 shadow multisig (0x4dbb), not the DAO.

evidence.shows: DeFiPunkd surfacer page for Controller confirming it is a verified Ethereum contract with owner, pendingOwner, pause, mint, burn, setFactory, setMembers surface
Controller owner() at block 25010861 returned 0x972Eed35781f09987a5c40F761f6A24623C570DE — NOT the disclosed DAO multisig 0xB33f8879

Controller ABI verified as contractName=Controller, proxy=null (immutable), 21 functions including mint, burn, pause, unpause, setFactory, setMembers, callTransferOwnership, token(), members(), factory(), getWBTC()
Controller pendingOwner() at block 25010864 returned zero address — no pending ownership transfer in progress
DeFiPunkd surfacer page for WBTC DAO Gnosis Safe multisig confirming getOwners and getThreshold endpoints available

DAO multisig getOwners() at block 25010865 returned exactly 13 addresses matching the DAO/README.md member list: Balancer (0x512f), BitGo (0x157b), RiskDAO (0xe27B), Krystal (0xE71B), Kyber (0x8180), Loopring (0x4CBb), Multichain (0xa80F), Ren (0xF54c), Tom Bean (0xcBf1), Chainlink (0x65CE), Badger (0x86cb), Gopax (0x6707), Compound (0x8B85)
DeFiPunkd surfacer page for deprecated DAO multisig confirming contract is on Ethereum
Deprecated DAO multisig ABI verified as contractName=MultiSigWalletWithDailyLimit, proxy=null, 26 functions; has required(), getOwners(), submitTransaction(), confirmTransaction()
DeFiPunkd surfacer page for shadow/Small-DAO multisig confirming contract is on Ethereum
Shadow multisig ABI verified as contractName=MultiSigWalletWithDailyLimit, proxy=null — same contract type as deprecated DAO multisig; has required(), getOwners()
DeFiPunkd surfacer page for BitGo custodian multisig confirming contract exists on Ethereum; ABI not available (404 from Etherscan)
DeFiPunkd surfacer page for Members contract confirming contract is on Ethereum with owner, addMerchant/removeMerchant/setCustodian registry functions
Members contract owner() at block 25010869 returned 0x4dbbbFb0e68bE9D8F5a377A4654604a62E851e80 — the shadow/Small-DAO multisig, NOT the 8-of-13 DAO multisig
DeFiPunkd surfacer page for Factory contract confirming contract is on Ethereum
Factory owner() at block 25010874 returned 0xCA06411bd7a7296d7dbdd0050DFc846E95fEBEB7 — the Controller; consistent with described control hierarchy

WBTC ERC-20 token at 0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599 confirmed on Ethereum, named 'Wrapped BTC: WBTC Token'; ContractCreator tagged 'Wrapped BTC: Deployer' at 0x8b41783ad99fcbeb8d575fa7a7b5a04fa0b8d80b; contract name=WBTC, Solidity 0.4.24, immutable (no proxy)

Official DAO README confirms: current DAO multisig 0xB33f8879 (Gnosis Safe), deprecated multisig 0xD409 (deprecated), WBTC token 0x2260fac5, Controller 0xca0641, Factory 0xe5a5f1, Members 0x3e8640; custodian BitGo at 0xb0f42d; 13 named DAO members with Ethereum addresses; 24 merchants listed. Derived: threshold 8-of-13 per this README context and GitHub PR #12.

Three 2018 audits identified: ChainSecurity (ChainSecurity_WBTC.pdf), Solidified (2018-11-19), Coinspect (WBTC2018v1113.pdf). Relevant deployed commit: 0e8c140ab5fa006a17afa504dafcf311f56f81a2. Audit scope covers WBTC token, Controller, Members, Factory.
WBTC token on Arbitrum One at 0x2f2a2543B76A4166549F7aaB2e75Bef0aefC5B0f; BeaconProxyFactory-style bridge architecture; tagged 'Wrapped BTC' by Arbiscan
WBTC token on Optimism at 0x68f180fcCE6836688E9084f035309E29Bf0A2095; L2 StandardERC20 with l1Token=0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599 and l2Bridge=0x4200000000000000000000000000000000000010
WBTC on Polygon at 0x1bfd67037b42cf73acf2047067bd4f2c47d9bfd6; UChildERC20 proxy, implementation 0x7ffb3d637014488b63fb9858e279385685afc1e2
WBTCOFT on Base at 0x0555E30da8f98308EdB960aa94C0Db47230d2B9c; contractName=WBTCOFT, LayerZero OFT, Solidity 0.8.22; separate from the Ethereum WBTC DAO governance

unknowns: D1: Etherscan searchHandler URL for WBTC name-tag not directly fetched; name-tag evidence sourced from search-result page bodies instead
D3-offchain: Audit PDFs (ChainSecurity_WBTC.pdf, Solidified 2018-11-19, Coinspect WBTC2018v1113.pdf) not directly fetched this run; referenced via audit_summary.md
D5: WBTC on BNB Chain, Linea, and other chains mentioned in Etherscan multichain data — zero on-chain verification attempted this run; addresses unknown

D7: WBTC DAO multisig 0xB33f8879 threshold not confirmed on-chain this run — getThreshold() returned 400 and /api/safe/owners returned 404; threshold 8-of-13 sourced from GitHub PR #12 and news reports (search snippets), not on-chain
D7: BitGo custodian multisig 0xb0F42D187145911C2aD1755831aDeD125619bd27 — no ABI available on Etherscan (404); actor_class and signer composition unverified this run

D8: 0x972Eed35781f09987a5c40F761f6A24623C570DE — current Controller owner per on-chain read; surfacer URL /address/1/0x972Eed... rejected; actor_class unknown; identity not established this run; next run should surface this address

D8: 0x8b41783ad99fcbeb8d575fa7a7b5a04fa0b8d80b — WBTC token deployer EOA per Etherscan page; surfacer URL rejected; actor_class eoa (inferred from Etherscan label 'Deployer') but not on-chain verified

D8: L2 token addresses (Arbitrum 0x2f2a2543, Optimism 0x68f180fc, Polygon 0x1bfd6703, Base 0x0555E30d) sourced from search result snippets; surfacer URLs rejected (non-defipunkd sources); governance/admin structure for each L2 deployment unverified
D8: 24 merchant addresses listed in WrappedBTC/DAO README not individually surfaced or verified on-chain this run; getMerchants() on Members contract not called

admin_addresses.role: owner — Controller; owns WBTC token, can mint, burn, pause, unpause, setFactory, setMembers; currently owned by 0x972E (not disclosed DAO multisig)
owner — current Controller owner per on-chain read; identity and actor_class not established this run (D8)

multisig — WBTC DAO Gnosis Safe; 13 confirmed owners (Balancer, BitGo, RiskDAO, Krystal, Kyber, Loopring, Multichain, Ren, Tom Bean, Chainlink, Badger, Gopax, Compound); reported threshold 8-of-13; does NOT currently own Controller per on-chain read
multisig — shadow/Small-DAO MultiSigWalletWithDailyLimit; current owner of Members contract; reported 5-of-7 with BitGo holding 3 of 7 keys
other — Members registry; addMerchant/removeMerchant/setCustodian; owned by shadow multisig 0x4dbb
factory — sole address allowed to call Controller.mint; handles merchant mint/burn requests; owned by Controller
multisig — BitGo custodian multisig; confirmMint authority; reportedly joint BitGo/BiT Global after 2024 custody transition; no ABI on Etherscan
multisig — deprecated DAO MultiSigWalletWithDailyLimit (was 11-of-18); replaced by 0xB33f in Nov 2022
other — EOA in historical WBTC ownership-claim and DAO-confirmation transaction paths
other — WBTC token deployer EOA (tagged 'Wrapped BTC: Deployer' on Etherscan); D8 surfacer rejected
token — WBTC bridged token on Arbitrum One; BeaconProxyFactory architecture; D8 surfacer rejected
token — WBTC L2 standard ERC-20 on Optimism; l1Token=0x2260; D8 surfacer rejected
token — WBTC UChildERC20 proxy on Polygon; D8 surfacer rejected
token — WBTCOFT LayerZero OFT on Base; D8 surfacer rejected

protocol_metadata.about: WBTC (Wrapped Bitcoin) is an ERC-20 token on Ethereum backed 1:1 by Bitcoin held in custody. Approved merchants deposit native BTC with the BitGo/BiT Global custodian, which then triggers the Factory contract to mint equivalent WBTC to the merchant; redemption reverses this flow. The protocol is governed by an 8-of-13 Gnosis Safe DAO whose 13 members include Kyber, Chainlink, Compound, Balancer, and BitGo; this multisig has authority to modify the Controller (which owns the token), replace the custodian, and pause/unpause token transfers. Since late 2024 Bitcoin custody has been co-managed by a BitGo/BiT Global joint venture linked to Justin Sun, and on-chain evidence shows the Controller's owner has changed to an address (0x972E) not matching the disclosed DAO multisig.
